page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-96GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, alongside 96.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. The instance does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance but weak single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core test scores 778.19, placing it in the bottom 10%, while the multi-core test reaches 6248.71, which is in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth is stable, with cached read-only small block speeds of 829.52 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds of 56.72 GB/sec. Geekbench scores show a single-core score of 1210.0 and a multi-core score of 5265.0.

Given its high memory density and moderate multi-core performance, this server is suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. Appropriate use cases include database operations, in-memory caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and software compilation. It is less optimal for tasks requiring high single-core throughput or complex image processing.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-96GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. This configuration offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications. While single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, scoring 778.19 in stress-ng benchmarks, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5265.0. The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage. It is best suited for workloads such as database operations, caching layers like Redis, and static web serving, where memory capacity is prioritized over raw single-threaded compute power.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 12.0 GB per core
